howItWorks.intro: This soil volume calculator uses established formulas to provide accurate results.
howItWorks.outro: Results are estimates based on standard formulas. Verify with current local data for your specific situation.

faq.question: How much soil for a 4x8 raised bed?
faq.answer: A 4×8 bed at 12" deep needs 32 cubic feet (1.2 cubic yards). That's about 43 bags of bagged soil, or a small pickup truck load of bulk. Most beds are 10-12" deep for vegetable gardening.

faq.question: What soil mix for raised beds?
faq.answer: The classic mix: 1/3 topsoil, 1/3 compost, 1/3 aeration (perlite, vermiculite, or coarse sand). Mel's Mix (Square Foot Gardening) uses 1/3 peat moss, 1/3 vermiculite, 1/3 blended compost.

faq.question: Is bulk or bagged soil cheaper?
faq.answer: Bulk is much cheaper: $25-45/cubic yard vs $5-8 per 40lb bag. One cubic yard equals about 36 bags — that's $180-290 in bags vs $25-45 bulk. Delivery usually costs $50-100. Bulk wins for anything over 1 cubic yard.

faq.question: Is the Soil Volume Calculator free to use?
faq.answer: Yes, completely free with no signup required. Use it as many times as you need — there are no limits or hidden fees.

faq.question: How accurate is this calculator?
faq.answer: This calculator uses standard gardening and horticulture formulas trusted by gardeners and growers. Results are reliable estimates for planning purposes. For critical decisions, we recommend consulting a qualified professional to verify.

sections.title: What Is the Soil Volume Calculator?
sections.body: The Soil Volume Calculator is a free online tool designed for gardeners and growers who need quick, accurate calculations in the gardening and horticulture space. By entering your length, width, depth, you get instant results including cubic feet, cubic yards, 40lb bags needed. No formulas to memorize, no spreadsheets to build — just enter your numbers and get the answer in seconds. Whether you're a beginner or experienced professional, this calculator saves you time and eliminates guesswork.

sections.title: Why This Calculation Matters
sections.body: Getting cubic feet right can make the difference between success and costly mistakes. In gardening and horticulture, small errors compound quickly. Manual calculations are error-prone and time-consuming, especially under pressure. This calculator applies proven formulas used by gardeners and growers worldwide, giving you confidence that your numbers are correct. Use it to grow a thriving garden with precision and avoid common pitfalls that trip up beginners.

sections.title: When Should You Use This Calculator?
sections.body: This tool is most useful when you know your length and need to find the right cubic feet. It's also great for quick estimates before committing to a decision, and to double-check manual calculations or professional quotes, and when comparing different scenarios side by side. Bookmark this page and come back whenever you need a fast, reliable answer — the calculator is always free and requires no signup.
